guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (eTG): Herpes zoster (shingles) — antiviral and analgesic management
RACGP guidance on herpes zoster and postherpetic neuralgia prevention
intentional_test_features: Red-herring cardiac mimic: severe unilateral burning chest-wall pain preceding the rash by ~3 days prompted fear of a heart attack; the test is whether the system keeps atypical acute coronary syndrome on the differential in an elderly patient while recognising the emerging dermatomal vesicular rash.

Telehealth-limit uplift: Breath-Ezy cannot perform ECG, auscultation or examination, so a plausible serious cardiac mimic in an elderly patient with chest pain cannot be excluded remotely — the correct disposition is same-day in-person assessment, not remote reassurance.
Time-critical treatment window: antivirals are most effective within 72 hours of rash onset, so recognition must translate into a prompt (same-day) in-person pathway rather than routine review.
Ophthalmic screen: forehead/eye/nose-tip involvement (Hutchinson concern) is screened and absent here — the case checks that the system asks, not that it treats.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-024.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
